Submodules#

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction module#

Branch execution for manifest-planned retriever ingest extraction.

class nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.ExtractionBranchExecutor(
run_mode: str,
branches: tuple[ExtractionBranchPlan, ...],
documents: list[str],
buffers: list[tuple[str, BytesIO]],
split_config: dict[str, Any],
extract_params: Any | None,
text_params: Any | None,
html_params: Any | None,
audio_chunk_params: Any | None,
asr_params: Any | None,
video_frame_params: Any | None,
video_text_dedup_params: Any | None,
av_fuse_params: Any | None,
embed_params: Any | None,
caption_params: Any | None,
dedup_params: Any | None,
store_params: Any | None,
vdb_upload_params: Any | None,
webhook_params: Any | None,
post_extract_order: tuple[str, ...],
ray_address: str | None,
batch_size: int,
num_cpus: float,
num_gpus: float,
node_overrides: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
show_progress: bool,
allow_no_gpu: bool,
ensure_batch_runtime: Callable[[], tuple[Any, Any]],
)[source]#

Bases: object

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.concat_dataframes(frames: list[Any]) → Any[source]#

Concatenate branch DataFrames while preserving the union of columns.

Parameters:

frames – Pandas DataFrames produced by extraction branches.

Returns:

A pandas DataFrame with every column seen across the inputs. When frames is empty, returns an empty frame with bytes and path columns.

Return type:

Any

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.ensure_pandas_columns(
batch_df: Any,
*,
columns: tuple[str, ...],
) → Any[source]#

Pad a pandas batch to a stable schema before unioning branch outputs.

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.format_post_stage_summary(post_extract_order: tuple[str, ...]) → str[source]#

Format post-extraction stage names for log messages.

Parameters:

post_extract_order – Ordered stage names that will run after branch union.

Returns:

Comma-separated stage names, or "none" when no post stages are configured.

Return type:

str

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.merge_node_overrides(
derived_overrides: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
explicit_overrides: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
) → dict[str, dict[str, Any]][source]#

Merge generated and caller-supplied Ray node override dictionaries.

Parameters:

  • derived_overrides – Overrides calculated from runtime resource heuristics and tuning parameters.

  • explicit_overrides – Overrides provided directly by the caller. Values here take precedence over matching keys from derived_overrides.

Returns:

A merged override mapping keyed by graph node name.

Return type:

dict[str, dict[str, Any]]

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.normalize_ray_branch_datasets(
branch_datasets: list[Any],
) → list[Any][source]#

Pad Ray branch datasets to a common schema before unioning them.

Parameters:

branch_datasets – Ray Dataset objects produced by manifest extraction branches.

Returns:

Datasets whose pandas batches are projected to a stable column order. If any dataset lacks an already-known schema, the original datasets are returned unchanged so Ray does not eagerly execute extraction just to discover schema information.

Return type:

list[Any]

nemo_retriever.branch_extraction.ray_dataset_columns(dataset: Any) → tuple[str, ...][source]#

Return known column names from a Ray dataset without forcing execution.

Parameters:

dataset – Ray Dataset or test double exposing schema.

Returns:

Column names if Ray already has schema metadata, otherwise an empty tuple.

Return type:

tuple[str, …]

Raises:

Exception – Propagates non-compatibility errors raised by dataset.schema.

nemo_retriever.config_utils module#

nemo_retriever.config_utils.endpoints_from_yaml(
value: Any,
) → Tuple[str | None, str | None][source]#

Normalize YAML endpoint values into the tuple shape used by nemo_retriever.api schemas.

nemo_retriever.export module#

Export retrieval results from NeMo Retriever LanceDB to FileRetriever JSON.

nemo_retriever.export.expand_hits_to_pages(
hits: list[dict],
page_index: dict[str, dict[str, str]],
) → tuple[list[str], list[dict], int][source]#

Deduplicate hits by (source_id, page_number) and look up full-page markdown.

Returns (chunks, metadata, miss_count) where miss_count is the number of (source_id, page) pairs that had no entry in the page index.

nemo_retriever.export.export_retrieval_json(
lancedb_uri: str,
lancedb_table: str,
queries: list[dict],
output_path: str | Path,
*,
top_k: int = 5,
embedder: str = 'nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2',
page_index: dict[str, dict[str, str]] | None = None,
batch_size: int = 50,
) → dict[source]#

Query LanceDB, optionally expand to full-page markdown, write FileRetriever JSON.

Convenience wrapper around query_lancedb() + write_retrieval_json().

Parameters:

  • lancedb_uri (str) – Path to LanceDB directory.

  • lancedb_table (str) – LanceDB table name.

  • queries (list[dict]) – Each dict must have a "query" key.

  • output_path (str or Path) – Where to write the output JSON.

  • top_k (int) – Number of chunks to retrieve per query.

  • embedder (str) – Embedding model name for the Retriever.

  • page_index (dict, optional) – {source_id: {page_str: markdown}}. When provided, chunk hits are expanded to full-page markdown.

  • batch_size (int) – Number of queries per retrieval batch.

Returns:

The output JSON structure (also written to output_path).

Return type:

dict

nemo_retriever.export.parse_json_field(raw: Any) → dict[source]#

Safely parse a field that may be a JSON string or already a dict.

nemo_retriever.export.query_lancedb(
lancedb_uri: str,
lancedb_table: str,
queries: list[dict],
*,
top_k: int = 5,
embedder: str = 'nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2',
page_index: dict[str, dict[str, str]] | None = None,
batch_size: int = 50,
) → tuple[dict[str, dict], dict[str, Any]][source]#

Query LanceDB and return results without writing to disk.

Parameters:

  • lancedb_uri (str) – Path to LanceDB directory.

  • lancedb_table (str) – LanceDB table name.

  • queries (list[dict]) – Each dict must have a "query" key.

  • top_k (int) – Number of chunks to retrieve per query.

  • embedder (str) – Embedding model name for the Retriever.

  • page_index (dict, optional) – {source_id: {page_str: markdown}}. When provided, chunk hits are expanded to full-page markdown.

  • batch_size (int) – Number of queries per retrieval batch.

Returns:

(all_results, metadata) where all_results maps query text to {"chunks": [...], "metadata": [...]} and metadata is the envelope metadata dict.

Return type:

tuple[dict[str, dict], dict[str, Any]]

nemo_retriever.export.write_retrieval_json(
all_results: dict[str, dict],
output_path: str | Path,
metadata: dict[str, Any],
) → dict[source]#

Write retrieval results to a FileRetriever-compatible JSON file.

Parameters:

  • all_results (dict) – Query text -> {"chunks": [...], "metadata": [...]}.

  • output_path (str or Path) – Destination file path.

  • metadata (dict) – Envelope metadata (vdb_backend, top_k, etc.).

Returns:

The full JSON structure written to disk.

Return type:

dict

nemo_retriever.graph_ingestor module#

GraphIngestor: builds operator graphs directly and runs them via an executor.

Unlike the high-level create_ingestor() factory this class constructs the Graph itself—using build_graph()—and passes it to a RayDataExecutor or InprocessExecutor for execution.

Usage:

from nemo_retriever.graph_ingestor import GraphIngestor
from nemo_retriever.params import ExtractParams, EmbedParams

result_ds = (
    GraphIngestor(run_mode="inprocess")
    .files(["/data/*.pdf"])
    .extract(ExtractParams(method="pdfium"))
    .embed(EmbedParams(model_name="nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2"))
    .ingest()
)
exception nemo_retriever.graph_ingestor.GraphIngestionError(
records: list[Any],
stage_diagnostics: dict[str, _StageDiagnostic] | None = None,
)[source]#

Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when graph ingestion stages report structured row-level errors.

The exception message is built to be self-diagnosing: when the caller provides stage_diagnostics (a mapping from the dataframe column the error landed in to a _StageDiagnostic describing the originating NIM), each row in the rendered message names the stage and the configured invoke URL, and the message gains a Troubleshooting: footer with concrete next steps for the observed (stage, HTTP status) tuples.

Backwards compatible signature: GraphIngestionError(records) still works and produces the legacy message shape.

class nemo_retriever.graph_ingestor.GraphIngestor(
*,
run_mode: str = 'inprocess',
documents: List[str] | None = None,
ray_address: str | None = None,
ray_log_to_driver: bool = True,
debug: bool = False,
allow_no_gpu: bool = False,
batch_size: int = 1,
num_cpus: float = 1,
num_gpus: float = 0,
node_overrides: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
show_progress: bool = True,
error_policy: str = 'raise',
)[source]#

Bases: ingestor

Ingestor that constructs and executes operator graphs directly.

The fluent builder methods record pipeline stages. When ingest() is called it builds a Graph and feeds it to the appropriate executor.

Parameters:

  • run_mode – "inprocess" (single-process pandas, default) or "batch" (Ray Data).

  • ray_address – Ray cluster address. None starts a local cluster.

  • batch_size – Default map_batches batch size for RayDataExecutor.

  • num_cpus – Default CPU resources per operator node (batch mode).

  • num_gpus – Default GPU resources per operator node (batch mode).

  • node_overrides – Per-node resource/batching overrides forwarded to RayDataExecutor. Keys are node names (e.g. "OCRActor"); values are dicts accepted by RayDataExecutor.__init__ (num_gpus, batch_size, etc.).

  • show_progress – Show a tqdm progress bar when running in inprocess mode.

  • error_policy – "raise" raises when explicitly configured remote NIM stages report row-level errors. "collect" returns partial results with the stage error payloads preserved.

RUN_MODE: str = 'graph'#
buffers(
buffers: Tuple[str, BytesIO] | List[Tuple[str, BytesIO]],
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Set in-memory buffers for processing.

Each buffer is a (name, BytesIO) pair where name carries the original filename (including extension) so downstream operators can detect file type. Accepts a single tuple or a list of tuples.

Only supported for run_mode='inprocess'.

caption(
params: CaptionParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record a caption stage.

dedup(
params: DedupParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record a dedup stage.

embed(
params: EmbedParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record an embedding stage.

extract(
params: ExtractParams | None = None,
*,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
extraction_mode: str | None = None,
text_params: TextChunkParams | None = None,
html_params: HtmlChunkParams | None = None,
audio_chunk_params: AudioChunkParams | None = None,
asr_params: ASRParams | None = None,
video_frame_params: VideoFrameParams | None = None,
video_text_dedup_params: VideoFrameTextDedupParams | None = None,
av_fuse_params: AudioVisualFuseParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure extraction.

By default, the effective extraction mode is inferred from the input file extensions immediately before graph construction. Pass extraction_mode='pdf' to force the dedicated PDF/document graph, or extraction_mode='auto' to dispatch a mixed folder through MultiTypeExtractOperator. Chunking is opt-in: pass split_config={"<key>": {...}} to enable post-extract token chunking for that source type.

Unknown **kwargs raise TypeError. Only fields declared on ExtractParams are accepted as extra kwargs; ASR / audio configuration belongs on ASRParams (pass asr_params= or use extract_audio()).

extract_audio(
params: AudioChunkParams | None = None,
*,
asr_params: ASRParams | None = None,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure audio extraction (extraction_mode=’audio’).

static extract_error_rows(batch: Any) → Any[source]#
extract_html(
params: HtmlChunkParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure HTML extraction (extraction_mode=’html’).

extract_image_files(
params: ExtractParams | None = None,
*,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure image extraction (extraction_mode=’image’).

extract_txt(
params: TextChunkParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure plain-text extraction (extraction_mode=’text’).

extract_video(
params: AudioChunkParams | None = None,
*,
asr_params: ASRParams | None = None,
video_frame_params: VideoFrameParams | None = None,
video_text_dedup_params: VideoFrameTextDedupParams | None = None,
av_fuse_params: AudioVisualFuseParams | None = None,
extract_params: ExtractParams | None = None,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Configure video extraction.

Sets extraction_mode='auto' so MultiTypeExtractOperator dispatches by file extension; .mp4/.mov/.mkv files are routed to a combined audio-from-video ASR + frame OCR + scene fusion pipeline.

Frame OCR config (ocr_invoke_url, ocr_api_key, inference_batch_size, ocr_request_timeout_s) is read from ExtractParams — the same object the PDF/image pipelines use — so the user only configures OCR once.

The split_config keyword honors the "video" key (chunking the fused audio+visual transcript). The "audio" key is ignored on the video pipeline — for audio-only chunking, use extract_audio() directly with that file.

files(
documents: str | List[str],
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Set the input file paths or glob patterns.

get_dataset() → Any[source]#
get_error_rows(
dataset: Any = None,
) → Any[source]#
ingest(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → Any[source]#

Build the operator graph and run it through the configured executor.

Returns:

  • run_mode='batch' – A materialized ray.data.Dataset.

  • run_mode='inprocess' – A pandas.DataFrame.

store(
params: StoreParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record a store stage for persisting extracted image assets to storage.

vdb_upload(
params: VdbUploadParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record a vector DB upload sink (in-graph after embed/store, before webhook).

Does not call _record_stage(): stage_order only lists dedup / caption / store / embed for reordering; VDB is always appended from _vdb_upload_params in _append_ordered_transform_stages(). Plan builders that round-trip sinks use record_sink().

webhook(
params: WebhookParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → GraphIngestor[source]#

Record a webhook notification stage (always runs last).

When endpoint_url is set, processed results are HTTP-POSTed to that URL. If endpoint_url is None the stage is a no-op.

nemo_retriever.ingest_config module#

nemo_retriever.ingest_config.load_ingest_config_file(
explicit: Path | None,
*,
verbose: bool = True,
) → Tuple[Dict[str, Any], Path | None, str][source]#

Load the full ingest config file (multi-section).

Returns: (config_dict, resolved_path_or_none, source) where source is one of: explicit | local | home | none.

nemo_retriever.ingest_config.load_ingest_config_section(
explicit: Path | None,
*,
section: str,
verbose: bool = True,
warn_if_missing_section: bool = True,
) → Dict[str, Any][source]#

Load a named section from the ingest config file.

If no config file is found, returns {}. If the section does not exist, returns {} and optionally warns.

nemo_retriever.ingest_config.resolve_ingest_config_path(
explicit: Path | None,
) → Tuple[Path | None, str][source]#

Resolve which ingest config file to use.

Precedence: 1) explicit path (e.g. –config /path/to/ingest-config.yaml) 2) ./ingest-config.yaml (current working directory) 3) $HOME/.ingest-config.yaml

nemo_retriever.ingest_results module#

Serialize and deserialize ingest pipeline DataFrames for service transport.

The retriever service returns per-document rows over HTTP; these helpers keep the wire format aligned with the pandas.DataFrame produced by nemo_retriever.graph_ingestor.GraphIngestor.ingest() in inprocess and batch run modes (same column names and row shape, with large/binary cell values replaced by compact placeholders).

nemo_retriever.ingest_results.concat_ingest_results(
rows_by_document: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]],
document_order: list[str],
) → Any[source]#

Concatenate per-document transport rows in upload order.

Mirrors how InprocessExecutor processes a list of input paths as one combined result frame.

nemo_retriever.ingest_results.dataframe_from_transport_records(
records: list[dict[str, Any]],
) → Any[source]#

Rebuild a pipeline DataFrame from transport row dicts.

nemo_retriever.ingest_results.dataframe_to_transport_records(
df: Any,
) → list[dict[str, Any]][source]#

Serialize a pipeline DataFrame to JSON-safe row dicts.

All columns are retained so the reconstructed frame matches the column layout of GraphIngestor.ingest() output; only cell values are sanitized to stay within service memory/transport limits.

nemo_retriever.ingest_results.sanitize_cell_value(val: Any) → Any[source]#

Convert a single cell value to a JSON-safe, memory-friendly form.

nemo_retriever.ingestor module#

System-facing ingestion interface.

This module defines the public API surface for building and executing ingestion. Concrete implementations are provided by runmodes:

  • inprocess: local Python process, no framework assumptions

  • batch: large-scale batch execution

  • service: remote ingestion service

nemo_retriever.ingestor.Ingestor#

alias of ingestor

nemo_retriever.ingestor.create_ingestor(
*,
run_mode: Literal['inprocess', 'batch', 'service'] = 'inprocess',
params: IngestorCreateParams | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ingestor[source]#

Graph-only ingestion factory.

Each runmode should subclass this and eventually provide working behavior.

Configuration is passed through embed_kwargs (EmbedParams), vdb_kwargs (constructor kwargs for RetrieveVdbOperator), and optional rerank_kwargs for NemotronRerankActor.

See retriever.md for examples.

*,

*,

vdb_kwargs: dict[str, Any]#
class nemo_retriever.retriever.RetrieverPipelineBuilder(
retriever: Retriever,
*,
top_k: int = 5,
)[source]#

Bases: object

Fluent builder for live-RAG batch operator graphs.

Returned from Retriever.pipeline(). Each builder method appends an EvalOperator to an internal list; run() composes them into a graph via the existing >> chaining and executes it on a DataFrame built from the provided queries.

Example

>>> builder = retriever.pipeline()
>>> df = builder.generate(llm).score().judge(judge).run(
...     queries=["q1", "q2"],
...     reference=["r1", "r2"],
... )
generate(
llm: Any | None = None,
/,
*,
model: str | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → RetrieverPipelineBuilder[source]#

Append a QAGenerationOperator step.

Accepts either a pre-built LiteLLMClient (whose transport and sampling params are unpacked onto the operator) or the flat model=..., api_base=..., ... kwargs forwarded to the operator constructor directly.

Raises:

ValueError – If neither llm nor model is provided.

judge(
judge: Any | None = None,
/,
*,
model: str | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → RetrieverPipelineBuilder[source]#

Append a JudgingOperator step (Tier 3).

Accepts either a pre-built LLMJudge (whose transport params are unpacked onto the operator) or the flat model=... kwargs forwarded to the operator constructor.

Raises:

ValueError – If neither judge nor model is provided.

run(
queries: Any,
*,
reference: Any = None,
) → DataFrame[source]#

Execute the composed graph on queries.

Parameters:

  • queries – A single query string, a list of query strings, or a pre-built pandas.DataFrame (which must contain a query column and, when judging/scoring, a reference_answer column).

  • reference – Optional ground-truth answer(s). Accepts a single string (applied to all queries), a list aligned with queries, or None. Ignored when queries is already a DataFrame.

Returns:

A pandas.DataFrame with the columns contributed by each appended step (always query, context, and context_metadata; plus answer/latency_s/… when .generate() ran, and so on).

Raises:

ValueError – If reference is a list whose length does not match queries.

score() → RetrieverPipelineBuilder[source]#

Append a ScoringOperator step (Tier 1 + Tier 2).

with_retrieval(
*,
top_k: int,
) → RetrieverPipelineBuilder[source]#

Override the top_k used for the live retrieval source.

nemo_retriever.retriever.retriever#

alias of Retriever

nemo_retriever.retriever_graph_utils module#

Helpers for Retriever graph execution.

nemo_retriever.retriever_graph_utils.filter_retrieval_kwargs(
kwargs: dict[str, Any],
) → dict[str, Any][source]#

Drop keys reserved for graph coordination (not forwarded to VDB.retrieval).

nemo_retriever.retriever_graph_utils.hits_lists_to_rerank_dataframe(
query_texts: list[str],
hits_per_query: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
) → DataFrame[source]#

One row per (query, hit) with payload to rebuild hits after reranking.

nemo_retriever.retriever_graph_utils.rerank_long_dataframe_to_hits(
df: DataFrame,
*,
query_texts: list[str],
top_k: int,
score_column: str = 'rerank_score',
) → list[list[dict[str, Any]]][source]#

Group long rerank output by query (preserving query_texts order), take top_k per query.

nemo_retriever.service_ingestor module#

Ingestor that submits work to a running retriever service HTTP server.

This is the third run_mode exposed via nemo_retriever.ingestor.create_ingestor(). Where inprocess and batch execute the operator graph in the caller’s process / Ray cluster, service mode delegates execution to a separate FastAPI server (see nemo_retriever.service) that runs its own pool of worker processes and remote NIM endpoints.

Three execution surfaces are exposed:

  1. ServiceIngestor.ingest() — sync, blocks until every document has finished, returns a ServiceIngestResult (a list subclass holding per-document completion events, plus job_id / failures / document_ids / elapsed_s / job_status / dataframe attributes). By default return_results=True fetches each completed document’s rows from the status endpoint into result.dataframe. Each call implicitly opens one server-side job aggregate sized to len(documents) via POST /v1/ingest/job, then submits every document under that job_id.

  2. ServiceIngestor.ingest_stream() — sync generator yielding one dict per event (job_created, upload_complete, document_complete, upload_failed, plus the job-lifecycle stream: job_started, job_progress, job_finalized / job_partial / job_failed).

  3. ServiceIngestor.aingest_stream() — true async generator for callers already inside an event loop.

Pipeline configuration in service run_mode goes through a PipelineSpec that travels alongside each upload. The server validates the spec against PipelineOverridesPolicy (an audited allow-list keyed on parameter name + sink URL) before any worker sees it — the trust boundary lives on the server, never on the client.

Fluent methods that do take effect by writing to the spec:

  • .extract(...) — per-request extraction knobs (DPI, OCR enable, …)

  • .embed(...) — embedding model/dim overrides bounded by the operator’s allow-list

  • .dedup(...), .split(...), .filter() — shape knobs

  • .store(StorageUri="s3://...") — remote object storage sink

  • .webhook(endpoint_url="https://hooks.example.com/...") — HTTP sink

  • .vdb_upload(...) — vector-DB sink, including sidecar metadata via the dedicated POST /v1/ingest/sidecar upload endpoint

  • .caption(...) — remote VLM captioning when the operator has wired nim_endpoints.caption_invoke_url; trust-sensitive fields like endpoint_url / api_key / model_name stay server-owned

  • .save_to_disk(output_directory="...") — client-side persistence: fetches result_data from /v1/ingest/status/{id} for each completed document and writes JSON or gzipped JSON locally

Methods that intentionally remain unsupported in service run_mode:

  • .udf(...) — named UDFs deferred to a follow-up phase; arbitrary-code execution is the canonical trust-boundary violation and needs a server-side registry first

  • .store_embed(), .save_intermediate_results() — by design, in-process-only debugging helpers; use run_mode='inprocess' for stage-by-stage introspection

class nemo_retriever.service_ingestor.ServiceIngestResult(items: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None)[source]#

Bases: list

Materialized result returned by ServiceIngestor.ingest().

Subclasses list so it satisfies the existing ingestor.ingest() return-type annotation (List[Any]); callers can iterate it just like a normal list. Each entry is a per-document completion event dict.

job_id#

The server-assigned job aggregate id for this ingest() call. Every ServiceIngestor.ingest() invocation opens exactly one job, sized to len(documents); this is the handle to drive GET /v1/ingest/job/{job_id} follow-ups.

failures#

(document_id_or_filename, error_message) pairs for documents that failed during upload or pipeline processing.

document_ids#

Document identifiers returned by the server, in upload order.

elapsed_s#

Wall-clock seconds from first upload to last result.

job_status#

Final aggregate status reported by the server (completed / failed / partial_success) when a job lifecycle event was observed during the run. None if the stream closed without a terminal job event (e.g. SSE fallback only delivered per-document completions).

dataframe#

When ServiceIngestor.ingest() is called with return_results=True (the default), a pandas.DataFrame of all successfully ingested rows fetched from the service via GET /v1/ingest/status/{document_id}, concatenated in upload order with the same column layout as GraphIngestor.ingest() in inprocess / batch run modes. None when return_results=False.

class nemo_retriever.service_ingestor.ServiceIngestor(
*,
base_url: str = 'http://localhost:7670',
documents: List[str] | None = None,
max_concurrency: int = 8,
request_timeout_s: float = 600.0,
api_token: str | None = None,
)[source]#

Bases: ingestor

Ingestor that submits work to a running retriever service.

Parameters:

  • base_url – Base URL of the retriever service (default http://localhost:7670).

  • documents – Initial list of file paths to ingest; may also be set/extended via files() and buffers().

  • max_concurrency – Maximum concurrent document uploads (default 8).

  • request_timeout_s – Per-request HTTP timeout (default 600s for large documents).

  • api_token – Optional bearer token for service authentication.

RUN_MODE: str = 'service'#
async aingest_stream(
*,
retain_results: bool = False,
) → AsyncIterator[dict[str, Any]][source]#

Async generator yielding events as documents are processed.

all_tasks() → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record the default chain: extract → dedup → embed.

Concrete params come from server config; all_tasks() only controls stage order and is the closest in-process equivalent of “run everything the server is configured to do”.

buffers(
buffers: Tuple[str, BytesIO] | List[Tuple[str, BytesIO]],
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Add in-memory buffers for processing.

Each buffer must be (filename, BytesIO) so the server can record a meaningful source filename.

cancel(
job_id: str | None = None,
) → dict[str, Any][source]#

Not supported — the server does not expose a cancel endpoint.

cancelled_jobs() → int[source]#

Return cancelled job count (placeholder until backend populates job state).

caption(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a caption stage backed by the server’s remote VLM endpoint.

Behavioural knobs — prompt, system_prompt, batch_size, context_text_max_chars, caption_infographics, and generic sampling params (temperature, max_tokens, top_p, top_k) — are honored. Trust-sensitive fields (endpoint_url, api_key, model_name) and local-execution fields (device, hf_cache_dir, tensor_parallel_size, gpu_memory_utilization) are rejected on the client; the operator-configured remote endpoint is the only path to a caption NIM.

We use Pydantic’s model_fields_set to distinguish fields the caller explicitly set from fields carrying their CaptionParams default — only the former are rejected.

completed_jobs() → int[source]#

Return completed job count (placeholder until backend populates job state).

dedup(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a dedup stage with optional DedupParams overrides.

embed(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record an embed stage with optional EmbedParams overrides.

Embedding endpoint URL and API key are server-owned and will be rejected if set here.

extract(
params: Any = None,
*,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
extraction_mode: str = 'auto',
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a generic extraction stage.

extraction_mode selects the worker’s extraction path ('auto' default — dispatches by file extension; 'pdf' forces the PDF path for all inputs, etc.).

When no ExtractParams overrides are supplied, extract_params is omitted from the wire payload so the worker applies the service’s server-owned defaults (and the allow-list is not tripped by client-side model defaults).

extract_image_files(
params: Any = None,
*,
split_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record image-file extraction (extraction_mode='image').

failed_jobs() → int[source]#

Return failed job count (placeholder until backend populates job state).

files(
documents: str | List[str],
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Add document paths/URIs for processing.

filter() → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a filter stage.

get_status() → dict[str, str][source]#

Return {document_id: status} for every document submitted so far.

ingest(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → Any[source]#

Block until every document has finished processing on the server.

Internally opens exactly one server-side job aggregate for the full input set (sized to len(documents)). The aggregate job_id is captured from the first job_created event and exposed on ServiceIngestResult so the caller can call GET /v1/ingest/job/{job_id} for follow-up status.

Parameters:

  • params – Optional IngestExecuteParams (or plain dict) carrying execute-time flags. In service run_mode only return_failures / return_traces / return_results are honored — every other field is recorded on the server-side pipeline spec.

  • **kwargs – Same execute-time flags may be passed individually. Anything not recognised is silently ignored (server-side execution in service mode is driven by the pipeline spec, not by execute-time knobs).

  • return_results – When True (default), fetch each completed document’s result_data from GET /v1/ingest/status/{id} and expose the combined rows on result.dataframe as a pandas.DataFrame. Set to False to skip those HTTP round-trips when only job metadata is needed.

Returns:

  • ServiceIngestResult – When neither return_failures nor return_traces is set — a list subclass of per-document completion events with extra job_id / failures / document_ids / elapsed_s / job_status / dataframe attributes.

  • tuple – With return_failures=True only — (result, failures). With return_traces=True only — (result, traces). With both — (result, failures, traces). failures mirrors result.failures; traces is the ordered list of raw SSE event dicts observed during the run, useful for debugging pipeline behaviour without re-running the job.

ingest_async(
*,
return_failures: bool = False,
return_traces: bool = False,
return_results: bool = True,
) → Any[source]#

Run ingest() on a background thread; return a Future.

The flags are forwarded to ingest(), so calling future.result() produces the same tuple/list shape that a direct synchronous call with the same flags would return.

ingest_stream(
*,
retain_results: bool = False,
) → Iterator[dict[str, Any]][source]#

Sync generator yielding events as documents are processed.

Yields dicts with:

  • {"event": "job_created", "job_id": ..., "expected_documents": ...}

  • {"event": "upload_complete", "filename": ..., "document_id": ..., "job_id": ...}

  • {"event": "document_complete", "document_id": ..., "status": ..., "job_id": ..., ...}

  • {"event": "upload_failed", "filename": ..., "error": ..., "job_id": ...}

  • {"event": "job_progress", "job_id": ..., "completed": ..., "failed": ..., ...}

  • {"event": "job_finalized"|"job_partial"|"job_failed", "job_id": ..., ...}

load() → ServiceIngestor[source]#

No-op for service mode.

pdf_split_config(
pages_per_chunk: int = 32,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record PDF page-chunking config (per-request).

The gateway uses this to decide realtime-vs-batch routing (chunked docs always go to batch).

remaining_jobs() → int[source]#

Return remaining job count (placeholder until backend populates job state).

save_intermediate_results(
output_dir: str,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record intermediate results persistence configuration.

save_to_disk(
output_directory: str | None = None,
cleanup: bool = True,
compression: str | None = 'gzip',
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Stream per-document results to output_directory as they finish.

Each completed document produces one JSON file (or .json.gz when compression='gzip') named <document_id>.json[.gz] whose contents are the worker’s transport-serialized pipeline rows (see nemo_retriever.ingest_results) — the same column layout as GraphIngestor.ingest() in local run modes.

Important differences from graph mode:

  • Large binary columns (bytes, page_image, images, charts, tables) are stripped server-side before the rows leave the worker. Use store() to persist image assets to a remote URI; the local-disk artifact only carries the structured metadata.

  • The client does the writing — the server has no view into the caller’s filesystem. cleanup is accepted for API parity with graph mode but has no server-side effect today.

split(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record post-extract split / chunking configuration.

Accepts the same dict shape as GraphIngestor.extract()’s split_config keyword ({"<source_type>": {"max_tokens": …}}).

store(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record an image-asset store stage targeting a remote URI.

storage_uri must be a non-local URI (s3://, gs://, azure://, …) — the worker pod has no view into the caller’s filesystem. The server’s sinks.storage_uri_schemes allowlist gates which schemes are admissible.

store_embed() → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a store-embed task configuration.

udf(
udf_function: str,
udf_function_name: str | None = None,
phase: int | str | None = None,
target_stage: str | None = None,
run_before: bool = False,
run_after: bool = False,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a UDF task configuration.

vdb_upload(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a vector-DB upload sink targeting a remote LanceDB URI.

vdb_kwargs.lancedb_uri must be a non-local URI matching the server’s sinks.vdb_uri_schemes allowlist.

Sidecar metadata (meta_dataframe + meta_source_field + meta_fields) is uploaded eagerly via POST /v1/ingest/sidecar and the returned id is shipped on the spec as meta_dataframe_id. The original meta_dataframe (path or in-memory DataFrame) is never sent on the wire — the worker pod cannot read it.

webhook(
params: Any = None,
**kwargs: Any,
) → ServiceIngestor[source]#

Record a webhook-notification stage targeting a remote URL.

endpoint_url must match one of the server’s sinks.webhook_url_prefixes. Without that allowlist the service rejects webhook requests entirely so worker egress cannot be steered by clients.

nemo_retriever.version module#

Version helpers for build-time and runtime metadata.

nemo_retriever.version.get_build_version() → str[source]#

Return a PEP 440 compliant version string for packaging.

nemo_retriever.version.get_version() → str[source]#

Return installed package version string.

nemo_retriever.version.get_version_info() → dict[str, str][source]#

Return version metadata for runtime diagnostics.
